test.name: Report impossibility and FamilySearch quality together, kept distinct
test.description: User asks to check Ole C. Hole (KD96-TV4). person_warnings fires hasEventAfterDeath1 (a 1975 Residence after his 1960 death -- a Fundamental impossibility), AND person_quality returns 2 quality issues. The skill should report BOTH, in two clearly-separate sections, without conflating them: the impossibility is Critical and gets the investigate-the-cause treatment; the quality issues are optional improvements and must NOT be escalated to error severity.

input.user_message: Check Ole C. Hole (KD96-TV4) for problems.

judge_context: Should call both person_warnings and person_quality with personId 'KD96-TV4'

Should report the hasEventAfterDeath1 warning as Critical (severity 'error') and recommend investigating the cause (identity confusion, wrong death date, or posthumous mention) before assuming one -- per the skill's event-after-death guidance
Should ALSO report the FamilySearch quality issues (~2: the death place missing a city, a residence with no tagged sources) in a SEPARATE section

Must keep the two distinct: the quality issues are improvements to the profile, NOT impossibilities. Must NOT label the quality issues as Critical/error, and must NOT merge them into the impossibility list
Should NOT re-derive either tool's output; report what the tools returned
